violations.text: An employee was observed touching ready to eat food (cut lemon wedges) with bare hands. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. PIC informed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2025

The auto dispenser for sink & surface concentration sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink a was dispensing at 0 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the sink & surface sanitizer must be 272/704-700/1875 ppm. Staff to manually dispense and test sanitizer until dispenser is served and working properly. New Violation. Correct By: 05/18/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine, slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris and/or biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Items taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. Ice disposed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2026
Cheese and house-mixed seasoned mayos were observed with date marks exceeding 7 days. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2026
Working containers used for storing chemicals (soap) taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 05/18/2026

Working containers of oils and spices are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 05/18/2026
Refrigerator door gaskets of the prep units were observed in poor repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/05/2024

An employee was observed touching ready to eat food (sliced bread) with bare hands. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. Employee was directed to wash hands and wear gloves, PIC informed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

Date of last shellfish served was not documented on shellfish tags. Shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

Cooked rice was observed held at 45 degrees Fahrenheit in the prep unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC chose to dispose during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024
A bowl of greens was observed stored in cut romaine lettuce in the salad prep unit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024
Cases of can goods were observed stored on the floor in the storeroom. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024
Gasket on prep unit door gaskets were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the slicer and stand mixer have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Staff cleaned and sanitized during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024
